# Test: apx external register and list commands
#
# This scenario verifies:
# 1. Registering an external API adds it to apx.yaml
# 2. Listing external APIs shows the registered APIs
# 3. Re-registering a duplicate ID is rejected
# 4. Filtering list by origin works

# Setup: Create a canonical repo
mkdir canonical
cd canonical
exec git init
exec apx init canonical --org=testorg --repo=apis --skip-git --non-interactive

# Register an external API
exec apx external register proto/google/pubsub/v1 --managed-repo github.com/Infoblox-CTO/apis-contrib-google --managed-path google/pubsub/v1 --upstream-repo github.com/googleapis/googleapis --upstream-path google/pubsub/v1 --description 'Google Pub/Sub API' --lifecycle stable
stdout 'Registered external API: proto/google/pubsub/v1'
stdout 'Managed:'
stdout 'Upstream:'
stdout 'preserve'
stdout 'external'

# Register a second external API
exec apx external register proto/google/api/v1 --managed-repo github.com/Infoblox-CTO/apis-contrib-google --managed-path google/api --upstream-repo github.com/googleapis/googleapis --upstream-path google/api --import-mode preserve
stdout 'Registered external API: proto/google/api/v1'

# List all external APIs
exec apx external list
stdout 'External APIs'
stdout 'proto/google/pubsub/v1'
stdout 'proto/google/api/v1'

# Try to register a duplicate - should fail
! exec apx external register proto/google/pubsub/v1 --managed-repo github.com/other/repo --managed-path other/path --upstream-repo github.com/googleapis/googleapis --upstream-path google/pubsub/v1

# List with origin filter
exec apx external list --origin external
stdout 'proto/google/pubsub/v1'
stdout 'proto/google/api/v1'

# Verify apx.yaml contains external_apis section
exec cat apx.yaml
stdout 'external_apis'
stdout 'proto/google/pubsub/v1'
stdout 'managed_repo'
stdout 'upstream_repo'
